title: Go 1.26 and AWS SDK for Go v2 Client Contract · Go Build Room
summary: A delivery team incrementally builds pinned Go and AWS SDK v2 client contract, changes one file or failure, and keeps earlier HTTP, Go, accessibility, security, and resource-budget invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of pinned Go and AWS SDK v2 client contract while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Pin Go 1.26.5, aws-sdk-go-v2 1.42.1, S3 1.105.0, CloudFront sign 1.11.7, and tested operating targets as one compatibility contract.
Load immutable shared configuration once, construct scoped clients, pass context, and assign transport and credential lifetimes.
Use SDK pointers, enums, document types, output metadata, paginators, and nil handling without losing API semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Runtime Sdk Version
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go file-delivery team handles case 7460 during a cost-anomaly investigation. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce pinned Go and AWS SDK v2 versions, client lifetime, runtime validation, and typed errors; cap bytes, time, attempts, concurrency, and spend before starting work; then change the object version and prove stale authority is rejected. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Runtime Sdk Version.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for pin go 1.26.5, aws-sdk-go-v2 1.42.1, s3 1.105.0, cloudfront sign 1.11.7, and tested operating targets as one compatibility contract. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Runtime Sdk Version, keep Runtime Sdk Version connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: storage-go-sdk-error-interface-zwlu2n" that uses case 7197 to enforce pinned Go and AWS SDK v2 versions, client lifetime, runtime validation, and typed errors. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import AWS SDK packages, open sockets, contact S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, networks, databases, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5 compile, vet, fuzz, race, leak, controlled AWS, load, fault, security, cost, restore, and production gates.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Sdk Error Interface.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Sdk Error Interface into observable performance evidence. content.text: The Sdk Error Interface structural check is only one gate. pinned Go and AWS SDK v2 client contract must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Sdk Error Interface
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go file-delivery team handles case 3367 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce pinned Go and AWS SDK v2 versions, client lifetime, runtime validation, and typed errors; preserve accessible progress, cancellation, status, and recovery evidence; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Inspect smithy operation, API, transport, cancellation, and response errors with errors.Is and errors.As while preserving request evidence.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?
